words.meaning_en: To look thoughtfully at something for a long time; to think deeply about something
words.example_sentence: He sat in silence to contemplate the meaning of his experience.

one_word_sub.question: To think deeply and seriously about something over a period of time

words.sentence: She paused to ______ the long-term consequences of her decision.

words.meaning_en: To delay or postpone action; to put off doing something
words.example_sentence: Students who procrastinate often struggle to complete assignments on time.

one_word_sub.question: To repeatedly delay or postpone doing something that needs to be done

words.sentence: If you continue to ______, you will miss every important deadline.

words.meaning_en: Done consciously and intentionally; to think carefully before deciding
words.example_sentence: The committee deliberated for hours before reaching a verdict.

one_word_sub.question: To think carefully and weigh all options before making a decision

words.sentence: The judge asked the jury to ______ carefully before delivering the final verdict.

words.meaning_en: To work jointly with others, especially in an intellectual endeavour
words.example_sentence: Scientists from different countries collaborated to develop the vaccine.

one_word_sub.question: To work together with others towards a common goal or project

words.sentence: The two departments agreed to ______ on the development of the new product.

words.meaning_en: To bring about or initiate an action or event; to provoke or stir up
words.example_sentence: The provocative speech was seen as an attempt to instigate unrest.

one_word_sub.question: To provoke, incite, or cause a person to do something, especially something wrong

words.sentence: The opposition accused the ruling party of trying to ______ communal tension.

words.meaning_en: To make a process happen sooner or be accomplished more quickly
words.example_sentence: The minister ordered officials to expedite the relief operations.

one_word_sub.question: To speed up or cause a task or process to be completed more quickly

words.sentence: We need to ______ the approval process to meet the project deadline.

words.meaning_en: To put a decision, plan, or agreement into effect
words.example_sentence: The committee voted to implement the new attendance policy immediately.

one_word_sub.question: To put a plan, decision, or policy into practical action

words.sentence: Authorities need to ______ stricter regulations on industrial waste disposal.

words.meaning_en: To make a problem, injury, or offence worse or more serious
words.example_sentence: Stress can aggravate existing medical conditions significantly.

one_word_sub.question: To intensify or make an already difficult or bad situation worse

words.sentence: Ignoring the early symptoms only served to ______ the patient's condition.
